title: viola cervatiana
intro: Viola cervatiana, also known as the Alpine pansy or Spring gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The plant is known to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, Viola cervatiana is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
